system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.

# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.

dim_lines = [l for l in r['output'].split('\n') if ':' in l and 'x' in l]

# Parse dims

        fname = parts[0].strip()

print("\n--- CATEGORY 2: Instagram-style images (1080x1350 IG portrait) ---")
for f in insta_jpgs:

    dim = dims.get(f, '?')
    # Instagram IDs tell us source
    name = f.replace('_n.jpg','')
    parts = name.split('_')
    print(f"  {f}  {dim}  {size}KB  — Instagram post (ID: {parts[0]})")

print("\n--- CATEGORY 3: UUID JPGs (likely achievement badges/certificates/screenshots) ---")

    else:

    # Full path size

print(f"Total UUID JPGs: {len(uuid_jpgs)}, total size: {total_size // 1024} KB")

# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`dayah.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

print(f"Badges: {len(badge_files)}")

# Delete the temp files we created earlier

    p = f'{path}/{f}'
    if os.path.exists(p):
        os.remove(p)

# Show whats left
remaining = sorted(f for f in os.listdir(path) if f.endswith('.jpg') or f.endswith('.png'))
print(f"\nRemaining files: {len(remaining)}")
for f in remaining:
    size = os.path.getsize(f'{path}/{f}') // 1024

  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. {"success": true, "name": "paragu-ai-builder", "file": "references/build-debug-patterns.md", "content": "# Build & Debug Patterns for Next.js 16 + Supabase Projects

## TypeScript Type Errors Due to Incomplete Database Types

**Problem:** The `src/types/database.ts` file in these projects typically only defines a subset of actual Supabase tables (e.g. `products`, `categories`, `orders`). New tables like `customers`, `events`, `tickets`, `ci_documents`, `blacklist` are NOT in the Database type. This causes TypeScript errors when calling `supabase.from('customers').update(...)` — TS infers the type as `never`.

**Fix:** Add `// @ts-nocheck` at the VERY FIRST LINE of any page/route/component that queries these undefined tables. Not after `'use client'` — it must be line 1.

```
// @ts-nocheck
'use client';
import { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase/client';
```

**Better fix (proper):** Expand `src/types/database.ts` to include ALL actual tables from the Supabase schema. The quick fix above is acceptable for rapid development but not production-permanent.

## Next.js 16 Build Quirks

### Middleware Deprecated
Next.js 16 deprecates `src/middleware.ts`. The warning reads:
```
⚠ The "middleware" file convention is deprecated. Please use "proxy" instead.
Learn more: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/middleware-to-proxy
```

The middleware still works at runtime, but this feature is being replaced. For now, keep using middleware.ts — plan to migrate to proxy when the API stabilizes.

### Node_modules Type Errors
The existing `node_modules` for Next.js 16 produces non-blocking type errors from Next's own type declarations:
- `Module '"/root/fun4me-store/node_modules/@types/react/index"' can only be default-imported using the 'esModuleInterop' flag`
- `Cannot find module 'react-server-dom-webpack/server'`

These are **Next.js 16 internal type issues**, not project errors. The build still succeeds (both compilation and static generation pass). Ignore these warnings.

### Standalone Output
Projects use `output: 'standalone'` in `next.config.ts` for Docker deployment. This produces a self-contained `.next/standalone/` directory.

## Docker Compose for Swarm

The `docker-compose.yml` used with `docker stack deploy` must use the Swarm-compatible syntax:

**DON'T** (Swarm refuses):
```
mem_limit: 512m
```

**DO** (Swarm accepts):
```
deploy:
  resources:
    limits:
      memory: 512m
```

Full `docker stack deploy` flow:
```bash
cd project && docker build -t fun4me:prod .
cd /root/fun4me-deploy && docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml fun4me
```

The compose file should point `build.context` to the actual project directory (e.g. `/tmp/fun4me-store` or `/root/fun4me-store`).

## de get_task Auth Failures

`delegate_task` with Anthropic/Claude models may return `401 User not found` errors. This happens when the provider API key doesn't have multi-model delegation scoped — fall back to building things directly in the main thread when delegation fails repeatedly.

Covers research → planning → 29 initial EPICs → audit → 100 expansion EPICs → autonomous execution. ## When to Use - Client needs a new business website from scratch - Taking over an existing website project with EPICs to execute - Need to batch-build 40+ pages autonomously ## Phase 1: Research & Planning ### Competitive Research (delegate 3 parallel subagents) 1. 35 top US/EU websites in the industry (features, design, tech) 2. 35 LATAM/local market websites (WhatsApp patterns, local conventions) 3. 30 best-in-class + platform analysis + SEO best practices ### Client USPs to Extract - Years of experience, ratings, credentials, education, affiliations, specializations ### Default Tech Stack (LATAM small business) | Component | Choice | Reason | |-----------|--------|--------| | Framework | Next.js 15 (App Router) | SSG/SSR, SEO | | Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Rapid dev | | CMS | Supabase | Free tier, client updates | | Hosting | Docker on VPS + Traefik | Cost-effective | | Chat | WhatsApp Business | **NON-NEGOTIABLE for LATAM** | ## Phase 2: EPIC Structure (29 initial) **Phase 0 (6):** Brand, Scaffolding, Database, Content, SEO Foundation, Accounts **Phase 1 MVP (7):** Layout, Homepage, About, Services, Contact, SEO Components, Deployment **Phase 2 V1 (5):** Individual Service Pages, Gallery, Testimonials, First Visit/FAQ, Admin Panel **Phase 3 V2 (5):** Blog, Appointments, Intake Forms, Social Integration, Analytics **Phase 4 Final (6):** Multilingual, AI Chatbot, Virtual Tour, Promotions, Advanced Dashboard, Hardening ## Phase 3: Post-Build Audit (ROAST Pattern) After Phase 1-2 is built, write a ROAST.md and commit. Always check: 1. **Data layer**: Hardcoded content vs CMS queries? (always hardcoded initially) 2. **Dead links**: Nav links to unbuilt pages? 3. **Bundle bloat**: framer-motion for CSS-able animations? (88% reduction proven: 44KB→5.5KB) 4. **Unused packages**: next-intl installed but unconfigured = dead weight 5. **Error handling**: error.tsx, loading.tsx, not-found.tsx exist? 6. **shadcn/ui**: Configured but zero components installed? 7. **JSON-LD**: Created but not imported into layout? 8. **Forms**: react-hook-form + zod + server actions ready? 9. **CI/CD**: Any quality gates? 10. **Docker**: Network config matches actual server? 11. **A11y**: Skip-to-content, focus traps, reduced motion? 12. **Legal**: Cookie consent, privacy policy, local data protection laws? Then generate 100 expansion EPICs across: foundation fixes, patient experience, marketing, operations, advanced tech, scale, community, data intelligence, advanced features, revenue. ## Phase 4: Autonomous Execution ### Strategy (proven: 108 epics, 58 pages, 8.2K LOC in one session) 1. **Build ALL frontend pages first** — 40-50 pages need zero external services 2. **Write 3-6 pages per `execute_code` call** (~5s each) 3. **`npm run build` after every batch** — catches errors immediately 4. **Git commit + push after every successful build** — atomic commits closing 2-6 epics each 5. **Close issues in batch**: `for i in X Y Z; do gh issue close $i; done` 6. **Defer backend** — Supabase, APIs, auth come after all pages exist ### What to build WITHOUT Supabase (58 pages proven) Content pages (about, services, FAQ, blog, pricing, education, emergency, kids, nervous patients), interactive tools (financing calculator, risk assessment quiz, symptom checker, treatment comparison, smile assessment, cost-by-country estimator), admin UI shells (dashboard, CRM, scheduling, inventory, revenue, analytics, knowledge base, WhatsApp templates, treatment plan builder), Google Ads landing pages (noindex), legal pages (privacy, terms), error states (error.tsx, loading.tsx, not-found.tsx), PWA manifest, security headers, CI/CD, sitemap, robots.txt, JSON-LD schemas, cookie consent, newsletter signup, referral program, review generation (NPS-gated), patient intake form, appointment booking (multi-step), waiting room digital display, WiFi captive portal, REST API skeleton. ### What REQUIRES Supabase (defer) Real data fetching, form persistence, image uploads, auth, CRUD, patient portal, automated emails, real-time scheduling, WhatsApp integration. ### What to write as SQL migration files (build alongside pages) Write the full schema + seed data as SQL files even before creating the Supabase project. This way deployment is: create project → run SQL → set env vars → deploy. Proven: 14 tables + RLS + triggers + seed in one migration file. ## GitHub Issue Creation ### Labels MUST exist first `gh issue create --label "foo"` **silently fails** if label doesn't exist — no issue created at all. Always: ```python labels = ["phase-5", "performance", "bugfix", "legal", "automation", "revenue", "marketing", ...] for label in labels: terminal(f'gh label create "{label}" --repo {repo} --color ededed --force') ``` ### Batch 25 issues per `execute_code` call 50-call limit per script. Each `gh issue create` ≈ 1.5s. 25 issues ≈ 40s. ### Keep bodies short, ASCII only No `→`, curly quotes, or special chars in `--body` strings — shell quoting breaks. ## Performance: CSS Over JS Animation Libraries Replace framer-motion with: ```typescript // lib/hooks/use-in-view.ts — 20 lines, 0 bundle cost "use client"; import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react"; export function useInView(options?: IntersectionObserverInit) { const ref = useRef(null); const [isInView, setIsInView] = useState(false); useEffect(() => { const el = ref.current; if (!el) return; const observer = new IntersectionObserver( ([entry]) => { if (entry.isIntersecting) { setIsInView(true); observer.unobserve(el); } }, { threshold: 0.1, rootMargin: "-50px", ...options } ); observer.observe(el); return () => observer.disconnect(); }, [options]); return { ref, isInView }; } ``` CSS: `@keyframes fade-in-up` + `.animate-fade-in-up` + `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`. Component usage: conditional classes with `transitionDelay` per index. ## Tailwind CSS v4: `@theme` Pattern (NOT v3 `@tailwind` directives) Tailwind v4 uses `@import "tailwindcss"` and `@theme {}` blocks — NOT the old `@tailwind base/components/utilities` directives. The installed PostCSS plugin `@tailwindcss/postcss` is v4-only. ### globals.css (v4 correct) ```css @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --color-primary: #0F0F0F; --color-secondary: #FF6B35; --color-surface: #1A1A1A; --color-foreground: #F5F5F5; --color-background: #0A0A0A; --font-heading: "Playfair Display", serif; --font-body: "Inter", sans-serif; } body { font-family: var(--font-body); color: var(--color-foreground); background: var(--color-background); } ``` ### Migration from v3 to v4 - Remove `@tailwind base/components/utilities` and `tailwind.config.ts` - Remove `@tailwindcss/postcss` config from `postcss.config.mjs` — it's auto-detected - Add `@import "tailwindcss"` at top of `globals.css` - Add `@theme {}` block with `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--radius-*` tokens - Color references become `text-foreground` (not `text-gray-900`), `bg-background` (not `bg-white`), `border-border` (not `border-gray-200`) - v3 `@apply` still works; v3 `@layer` directives are ignored (use plain CSS instead) ### `tailwind.config.ts` is dead in v4 Do NOT create or maintain `tailwind.config.ts`. v4 ignores it completely. All config lives in `globals.css` `@theme {}` and `@utility {}`. If you see a postcss build error referencing `./app/globals.css` + `./app/layout.tsx`, it means the CSS has v3 syntax (`@tailwind base`) that v4's PostCSS plugin can't parse. ### Common v4 Build Error ``` Error: Turbopack build failed with 1 errors: ./app/globals.css → ./app/layout.tsx Expected '' # Change to avoid f-string collision: f'' # dangerouslySetInnerHTML (quadruple braces needed) f'dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{{{ __html: JSON.stringify(jsonLd) }}}}' ``` ### Wrong — produces literal `${...}` in HTML ```python f'' # Produces: className="${montserrat.variable} ${playfair.variable}" ``` ### Rule of thumb Avoid JS template literals (`\`...${var}...\``) inside Python f-strings. Use string concatenation or className joining instead. For `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, count braces: Python `{{}}` → JSX `{}` → inner `{{}}` → final `{{ __html: ... }}` → **need `{{{{ }}}}` in Python**. ## Service Auto-Generation Data Bug The auto-generated `services.tsx` references `cat.category` but the JSON data uses key `"name"`: ```typescript // WRONG — renders "undefined"

{cat.category}

// RIGHT const cats = [{ "name": "Memberships", items: [...] }]

{cat.name}

``` Always verify that the JSX accessor key matches the JSON object key when auto-generating component code. ## Client Type: Creative Agency / Portfolio Site For creative agencies, production houses, and portfolio-based businesses, use a DIFFERENT template than standard 10-page business sites: ### Key Design Differences | Feature | Standard Business | Agency | |---------|------------------|--------| | Theme | Light, white | Dark (near-black + accent color) | | Hero | Gradient or image | **Full-screen showreel video** | | Primary pages | Services, Contact, About | **Portfolio, Case Studies, Clients** | | Trust signals | Testimonials, years | **Client logos, work samples** | | Navigation | Simple links | **Filter + category system** | | Language | Spanish only | **ES + EN bilingual** | | Content type | Static text | **JSON-based portfolio data** | | CTAs | "Reservar", "Contactar" | "Ver Portfolio", "Proyecto similar" | | Standard pages | 10 | **15+ (10 case studies minimum)** | ### Dark Theme Palette ```css --color-primary: #0F0F0F; /* near-black */ --color-secondary: #FF6B35; /* energetic orange */ --color-surface: #1A1A1A; /* dark cards */ --color-surface-light: #2A2A2A; /* hover states */ --color-background: #0A0A0A; /* page background */ --color-foreground: #F5F5F5; /* light text */ ``` ### Portfolio Data Pattern Store projects as JSON files in `content/portfolio/`: ```json { "title": "Project Name", "client": "Client Name", "category": "video|photo|brand|events|marketing", "year": "2025", "description": "ES description", "approach": "How we tackled it", "results": "What was achieved" } ``` Portfolio pages use `generateStaticParams()` reading from the JSON directory for SSG. ### Minimum Page Set for Agencies - Home (showreel hero + featured work + client logos) - Portfolio (filterable grid with URL-based filter) - Portfolio/[slug] (case study with description, approach, results, gallery, "quiero algo similar" CTA) - Services (expandable service cards) - About (team + values + story) - Clients (logo wall + testimonials) - Blog + Blog/[slug] - Contact (form + WhatsApp + map) - FAQ - Legal pages ### i18n for Agencies (ES/EN) Many PY agencies serve international clients. Implement language toggle early: - Content in `content/es.json` and `content/en.json` - URL-based switching: `/en/portfolio`, `/en/contact` - Detect via `Accept-Language` header on first visit, persist in localStorage - Store as simple JSON key-value pairs, not next-intl (too heavy for small sites) ## Technical Pitfalls ### Service-at-Home Business Model For mobile beauty/wellness services (no fixed location, owner travels to client), see `references/service-at-home-business-model.md`. Key differences from e-commerce: session-based pricing, prep instructions, coverage zones, zero-cost marketing via owner's existing job (Uber). ### shadcn/ui duplicate keyframes `npx shadcn@latest add accordion` appends duplicate `keyframes`/`animation` in `tailwind.config.ts`. TypeScript fails: "multiple properties with same name." Fix: deduplicate immediately. ### Next.js 15 async params `params` are `Promise<{ slug: string }>`. Must `await params`: ```typescript export default async function Page({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) { const { slug } = await params; } ``` ### HTML comments in JSX `` breaks JSX compilation silently. Use `{/* */}`. Watch for this in generated template code. ### `` placement in layout.tsx In Next.js App Router, `` must be INSIDE ``, not before it. Putting `` before `` causes webpack syntax errors. Prefer the `metadata` export for most head tags; use `` child of `` only for things like ``. ### Don't use fake names in placeholder reviews Use "Paciente Verificado" / "Verified Patient" — not fabricated names. ### Docker network mismatch `docker-compose.yml` network must match the actual VPS setup. If VPS uses Swarm (`agent-net` overlay), standalone containers need a bridge network + manual Traefik connection. ### Docker healthcheck IPv6 trap Alpine-based images resolve `localhost` to `::1` (IPv6), but Next.js standalone binds to `0.0.0.0` (IPv4 only). Use `127.0.0.1` explicitly: ```yaml healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/health"] # NOT localhost ``` Traefik v3 **skips unhealthy/starting containers** — if healthcheck fails, the route never appears. Debug with: `docker exec $(docker ps -q -f name=traefik) cat /var/log/traefik/traefik.log | grep "Filtering unhealthy"` ### Traefik Docker provider label for standalone containers When Traefik runs in Swarm mode (`--providers.swarm=true`) but also discovers standalone containers (`--providers.docker.endpoint`), you MUST add the `traefik.docker.network` label so Traefik knows which network to use: ```yaml labels: - "traefik.docker.network=myproject_mynet" # full compose-prefixed name ``` ## Phase 5: Post-Launch Fix Audit After the site is deployed and running, audit these common issues: ### Address/Location Completeness The `constants.ts` address object almost always ships with `street: ""`. Fix requires updating ALL locations that render address: - `lib/constants.ts` — source of truth - `components/layout/footer.tsx` — footer address display - `components/home/location.tsx` — homepage map section - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — contact page - `components/seo/json-ld.tsx` — structured data (Google) ### Google Maps Embed Coordinates Generic `!2d-57.58!3d-25.28` coordinates point to wrong location. Fix: 1. Geocode the actual address via Nominatim: `curl -s 'https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/search?q=NEIGHBORHOOD+CITY+COUNTRY&format=json&limit=1' -H 'User-Agent: AppName/1.0'` 2. Update ALL iframe `src` attributes (search for `maps/embed` across codebase) 3. Update JSON-LD geo coordinates to match 4. Use `execute_code` with `search_files` to find ALL occurrences first ### Server Action Errors (Next.js 15) `[Error: Failed to find Server Action "x"]` in logs is from bots/crawlers sending invalid POST requests. This is a known Next.js 15 framework issue — NOT a code bug. Don't waste time investigating. ### Supabase Placeholder Pattern API routes (contact, newsletter, appointment) use try/catch around Supabase calls with `console.warn("Supabase not configured")`. This is correct — forms work (return success) even without a real Supabase project. Wiring real Supabase is a separate task requiring: create project → run migrations → update .env → rebuild. ### Quick Audit Commands ```bash # Find all address displays grep -rn "address\.\(city\|street\|country\)" components/ app/ --include="*.tsx" # Find all map embeds grep -rn "maps/embed\|maps.embed" --include="*.tsx" -l # Find all coordinate references grep -rn "latitude\|longitude\|-25\." --include="*.tsx" # Check for placeholder env vars grep "placeholder" .env # Check Docker logs for real errors (ignore Server Action noise) docker compose logs --tail=50 | grep -v "Server Action" ``` ## Adult Store / Sensitive Products Vertical (reference file) See `references/adult-store-ecommerce-patterns.md` for detailed patterns when building: - Adult stores (sex toys, lingerie, BDSM) - Any privacy-sensitive e-commerce - WhatsApp-first LATAM small business stores ### Critical Differences from Regular E-Commerce 1. **Privacy is the product**: Age gate, quick-exit, privacy mode, anonymous reviews, discreet packaging — these are feature requirements, not nice-to-haves 2. **WhatsApp is the checkout**: No cart, no payment gateway, no user accounts. Every "buy" button opens WhatsApp with prefilled message 3. **Content is educational, not titillating**: Beginner guides, safety info, material comparisons, care instructions 4. **SVGs replace product photos**: Until real photography is available, branded geometric SVGs with the site's color palette look professional instead of empty 5. **Safety badges build trust**: "Body Safe", "Hipoalergénico", "Impermeable" — these are conversion drivers 6. **Per-product FAQ**: Every product needs 2-3 Q&As (beginner-friendly, sound, cleaning, materials) 7. **Pricing: PYG only**: Dual-currency (PYG + USD) may be needed near borders but default to local currency ### Post-Build Checklist (add to Phase 3 Roast) 1. Promo carousel text spacing — no words smashed together 2. Blog content — full paragraphs, not truncated 3. Stats animation — starts from "—" not "0" 4. Pattern: - Wrong: `className="grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3"` → 1 col on mobile - Right: `className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3"` → 2 cols on mobile - Applies to featured products, blog index, promotions, envio points, testimonials, skeleton loader, and any grid of cards - Also applies to `grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2` (no lg variant) — same fix: add `grid-cols-2` base - `search_files(pattern='grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2', ...)` finds all offenders ## Key Principles 1. **Don't skip Phase 0** — client photos/content are always the bottleneck 2. **Claim Google Business Profile FIRST** — generates leads before website exists 3. **WhatsApp is mandatory for LATAM** 4. **MVP must go live in 2 weeks max** — perfectionism kills momentum 5. **Blog content can be AI-assisted** but must be client-reviewed 6. **Don't overbuild booking** — in LATAM, WhatsApp confirmation is the norm 7. **Professional photography > stock photos** for local businesses 8. **SEO takes months** — set expectations; GBP is the quick win 9. **Build pages first, wire data later** — proven most efficient approach 10. **Close process/config epics as you go** — directory submissions, ad account setup, social media creation are tasks for the client, not code. Close them with notes. 11. **Admin shells count** — building the empty UI (tables, filters, KPI cards) with "Conectar Supabase" placeholders is legitimate progress.
